body_txt: A popular choice for a character that prefers to remain anonymous, be it a mercenary with a Nom de Guerre or The Don of a Generic Ethnic Crime Gang , is known only as "The [nationality]" often specifically With a "The" . This establishes them as mysterious and as an outsider to the setting, and often signals a fearsome reputation , especially if the name is of a conflict-prone area. Furthermore, it may also invoke Deliberate Values Dissonance and N-Word Privileges if it's being used by the character in a derogatory manner. Sometimes the alias is a Non-Indicative Name , either as a deliberate misdirect or through Accidental Misnaming by associates because of Global Ignorance . This trope doesn't include common standalone names that also double as nationalities, such as Dutch, Finn, Turk etc, unless they are referring to them as nationalities, i.e. "The Dutch", "The Finn", "The Turk" , etc. See also Lord Country , Captain Patriotic , Captain Geographic , Hometown Nickname , and Race-Name Basis . Subtrope to Ominous Mundanity .
